---
title: "Fire & Ice"
description: "Wildfire, permafrost, and the retreat of Arctic sea ice"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import CANADA_COLOR, get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map, choropleth_groups_map, choropleth_categorical, bubble_map

MAP_CONFIG = {"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True,
              "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
prov_geo_path, prov_csv = "data/geo/prov_2021.geojson", "data/geo/statcan_prov_geography.csv"
```

## Fire and ice

### Wildfire

Wildfire is part of the boreal landscape, but the area burned varies enormously from year to year — and **2023 was, by a wide margin, the worst fire season on record**, with about **17.6 million hectares** burned across Canada (roughly the size of Florida). The chart shows the national total each year since 1959.

```{python}
#| echo: false
wf_path = "data/geography/nfdb_wildfire.csv"
if os.path.exists(wf_path):
    w = pd.read_csv(wf_path)
    ca = w[w["geography"] == "Canada"].sort_values("year").copy()
    ca["mha"] = ca["area_burned_ha"] / 1e6
    fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
        x=ca["year"], y=ca["mha"], marker_color=CANADA_COLOR,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{x}</b><br>%{y:.1f} million ha burned<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(
        plot_bgcolor="white", height=420,
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(title="Million hectares burned", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        margin=dict(t=30, b=70),
        annotations=[dict(text=f"Source: Natural Resources Canada, Canadian National Fire Database | Data as of: {get_data_date(wf_path)}",
                          xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, showarrow=False,
                          font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "displayModeBar": False})
else:
    print("Wildfire data not yet available — run the data pipeline.")
```

In that record 2023 season, the burning was concentrated in the boreal forest. The map below plots **every wildfire recorded that year as a single point, sized by the area it burned** — zoom and pan to your region. A handful of enormous fires in Quebec, the Northwest Territories and British Columbia account for most of the 17.6 million hectares, while thousands of smaller fires speckle the rest of the country. (Marker area is proportional to hectares burned, so the giant fires stand out from the many small ones.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
fp_path = "data/geo/nfdb_fire_points_2023.csv"
if os.path.exists(fp_path):
    fp = pd.read_csv(fp_path)
    fig = bubble_map(fp, "lat", "lon", "size_ha", color="#e3492a",
        customdata=fp[["province", "size_ha", "cause"]].to_numpy(),
        hovertemplate="<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>%{customdata[1]:,.0f} ha burned · %{customdata[2]}<extra></extra>",
        center={"lat": 60, "lon": -100}, zoom=2.3,
        source_note="Source: NRCan Canadian National Fire Database (NFDB), 2023 fire points; marker area ∝ hectares burned.")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Wildfire-points data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

### Permafrost

Much of the North sits on **permafrost** — ground that stays frozen year-round. It grades from **continuous** permafrost across the High Arctic (frozen almost everywhere) to **isolated patches** near its southern limit. The classic national map below shows those zones; note that it reflects mapping from the mid-1990s, and permafrost extent has been **retreating** as the climate warms, so the southern boundary today sits further north.

```{python}
#| echo: false
pf_geo_path, pf_csv = "data/geo/permafrost_zones.geojson", "data/geo/permafrost_zones.csv"
PF_COLORS = {
    "Continuous (90–100%)": "#08519c", "Extensive discontinuous (50–90%)": "#3182bd",
    "Sporadic discontinuous (10–50%)": "#6baed6", "Isolated patches (0–10%)": "#bdd7e7",
}
if os.path.exists(pf_geo_path) and os.path.exists(pf_csv):
    pf_geo = json.load(open(pf_geo_path))
    pf = pd.read_csv(pf_csv, dtype={"fid": str})
    fig = choropleth_categorical(pf_geo, pf, "fid", "zone",
        color_map=PF_COLORS, ordered=list(PF_COLORS.keys()), legend_orientation="h",
        center={"lat": 64, "lon": -96}, zoom=2.2,
        source_note="Source: NRCan, Atlas of Canada 5th ed. — permafrost (~1995; extent has since declined).")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Permafrost data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

### Arctic sea ice

The clearest single signal of a warming North is the decline of **Arctic sea ice**. Its extent swings with the seasons — peaking in March, bottoming out in September — but the **September minimum has fallen by roughly 40% since satellite records began in 1979**. Canada's Arctic waters, including the Northwest Passage, are increasingly ice-free at the end of summer.

```{python}
#| echo: false
si_path = "data/geography/nsidc_sea_ice.csv"
if os.path.exists(si_path):
    s = pd.read_csv(si_path)
    sep = s[s["month"] == 9].sort_values("year")
    mar = s[s["month"] == 3].sort_values("year")
    fig = go.Figure()
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=mar["year"], y=mar["extent_mkm2"], name="March (winter maximum)",
                             mode="lines", line=dict(color="#9ecae1", width=2)))
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sep["year"], y=sep["extent_mkm2"], name="September (summer minimum)",
                             mode="lines+markers", line=dict(color="#08519c", width=3), marker=dict(size=4)))
    fig.update_layout(
        plot_bgcolor="white", height=460, hovermode="x unified",
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        yaxis=dict(title="Sea-ice extent (million km²)", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero"),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="left", x=0),
        margin=dict(t=40, b=70),
        annotations=[dict(text=f"Source: NSIDC Sea Ice Index (G02135), Northern Hemisphere | Data as of: {get_data_date(si_path)}",
                          xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, showarrow=False,
                          font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Sea-ice data not yet available — run the data pipeline.")
```

## Download data

- [Wildfire area burned, national & by province (CSV)](../data/geography/nfdb_wildfire.csv) — NRCan Canadian National Fire Database
- [Arctic sea-ice extent, monthly (CSV)](../data/geography/nsidc_sea_ice.csv) — NSIDC Sea Ice Index (G02135)
